tags.name: Pricing
tags.description: Atlas-powered pricing recommendations and per-listing strategy. Recommendations are pre-computed by the Atlas pricing model from comp data, demand signals, and event calendars; this surface lets you read them, apply or decline them, and tune the strategy that constrains the model.

tags.name: Markets
tags.description: Atlas market intelligence — per-city KPIs, comp distributions, calendar-level demand, and events. Sourced from Atlas (Vanio's 660-worker market-intelligence fleet) merged with the customer's own listing data.

tags.name: Atlas
tags.description: Per-listing Atlas intelligence — comp set with daily nightly pricing, and Atlas DNA segment intelligence (quality tiers, design styles, ADR uplift recommendations). Sourced from Atlas's comp database + DNA scoring pipeline.

tags.name: Billing
tags.description: Plan info, usage, and checkout

tags.name: System
tags.description: Health checks and API status

tags.name: schema
tags.description: Custom field-mapping schemas to reshape response payloads to your app's data model. Built-in schemas (`native`, `calry`, `calry-v1`) ship out of the box; create custom schemas with `POST /v1/schema/custom` and select one per request via the `X-Schema` header.

bearerAuth.description: API key — `sk_test_*` for sandbox, `sk_live_*` for production

Property.description: A vacation rental property from a connected PMS

Reservation.description: A booking/reservation from a connected PMS. Identical shape between list-row (`GET /v1/reservations`) and detail (`GET /v1/reservations/{id}`) — SDK consumers can use the same type for both.

listingId.description: Internal Repull listing ID this reservation is on.

guestId.description: Internal Repull guest ID. Use `GET /v1/guests/{id}` for the full profile.

platform.description: Booking source. Lowercase. May be null on legacy rows.

totalPrice.description: Decimal-as-string (precision 10, scale 2) to preserve precision across mixed-currency totals.

confirmationCode.description: Channel-side confirmation code (Airbnb HMxxx, Booking.com numeric, etc.).

guestDetails.description: Raw guest details from the source channel (firstName, lastName, email, phone, count, etc.). Shape varies by platform — use the dedicated guest endpoint for a normalized profile.

createdAt.description: When the reservation row was created in Repull (not the booking-on-channel timestamp).

guestName.description: Pre-resolved display name (`firstName lastName`) extracted from `guestDetails`. Null when no first name is available.

Guest.description: Guest list-row shape returned by `GET /v1/guests`. Pre-resolved primary phone/email + display name + cumulative stay aggregates so list UIs can render without a per-row round-trip.

displayNameLong.description: Long display name (first + last). Falls back to displayName when last name is missing.

country.description: Guest country (from profile metadata or address).

phone.description: Primary phone contact (or first non-primary if no primary set).

totalRevenue.description: Decimal-as-string to preserve precision across mixed-currency totals.

GuestContact.description: One contact channel attached to a guest profile (phone, email, etc.).

GuestFlag.description: A risk/operational flag attached to a guest profile (e.g. blacklist, do-not-host, VIP). Severity comes from main vanio's flag taxonomy.

GuestReservationsSummary.description: Aggregate counts of reservations attached to the guest. `future` is derived from `total - past - cancelled`.

GuestProfile.description: Full guest profile returned by `GET /v1/guests/{id}`. Aggregates the base list-row fields plus contacts, flags, notes, risk metadata, and a reservations-summary rollup.

Conversation.description: Channel-agnostic message thread between the host workspace and a guest. Returned by `GET /v1/conversations`. The `id` is the internal Repull thread id (integer) — pass it back as the `{id}` path param on detail / messages calls.

subject.description: Thread subject (email/website channels) or null when not applicable.

lastMessagePreview.description: Short preview of the most recent message body for list-UI rendering.

status.description: `archived` is reserved for a future bit on `message_threads` — currently always `open`.

ConversationHost.description: Linked host metadata for a conversation thread. Currently populated for Airbnb threads (resolved through `airbnb_hosts`); null for other channels until per-channel host enrichment lands.

ConversationGuest.description: Linked guest metadata for a conversation thread. Resolved through the thread's `reservation_id` → `reservations.guest_id`. Up to 50 contacts are returned.

ConversationDetail.description: Returned by `GET /v1/conversations/{id}`. Extends the list-row `Conversation` shape with expanded `host` + `guest` blocks so SDK consumers can render thread headers without an extra round-trip.

Message.description: A single message inside a conversation thread. Returned by `GET /v1/conversations/{id}/messages`. `direction` is normalized to `inbound` (from the guest) / `outbound` (from the host or an automation).

externalMessageId.description: ID assigned by the source channel (Airbnb message id, Booking message id, etc.). Stable across syncs.

senderType.description: Free-form sender role from the channel (e.g. `guest`, `host`, `system`, `airbnb`). Use `direction` for binary inbound/outbound logic.

body.description: Message body in the original language.

translatedBody.description: English translation when the original language is non-English and a translation has been computed.

isAutomated.description: `true` when the message was sent by a Vanio automation (template, schedule, etc.).

aiGenerated.description: `true` when the body was authored by Vanio AI (autopilot, draft).

ReviewCategory.description: One scored sub-category of a multi-axis review (cleanliness, communication, accuracy, etc.). Categories vary by platform.

rating.description: Per-category rating on the platform's scale (typically 1..5).

ReviewResponse.description: Host response to a review, when present.

Review.description: A guest or host review unified across channels. Returned by `GET /v1/reviews` and `GET /v1/reviews/{id}`. Populated from main vanio's unified `reviews` table after the per-channel backfill cron has run.

id.description: Internal Repull review id — pass back to `/v1/reviews/{id}`.

externalId.description: ID in the source channel (Airbnb review id, Booking review id, etc.).

listingId.description: Internal Repull listing id the review is attached to.

reservationConfirmationCode.description: Channel-side confirmation code for the reservation being reviewed.

reviewerRole.description: Who wrote the review — `guest` (about the host/property) or `host` (about the guest).

rating.description: Overall rating on the platform's scale (typically 1..5). May be `null` for review types that lack a numeric overall score.

publicReview.description: Public-facing review text shown on the listing page.

privateFeedback.description: Private feedback the reviewer sent only to the host.

isRevieweeRecommended.description: Did the reviewer recommend the reviewee? Used for guest-side reviews.

expiresAt.description: When the review window closes (Airbnb has a 14-day window after checkout).

language.description: Detected language (ISO 639-1) of the review body.

host.description: Host metadata for the linked account. Currently populated for Airbnb only; null for other providers.

ConnectHost.description: Public-facing metadata about the host whose account is linked. Lets clients render an account-level card (avatar + name) instead of just an opaque ID. Email is intentionally NOT exposed for Airbnb — the partner API doesn't return host email.

displayNameLong.description: Preferred long-form name. Falls back to displayName when the host hasn't set a preferred form.

ConnectProvider.description: A channel the multi-channel Connect picker can show. Returned by `GET /v1/connect/providers` and consumed by SDKs that render their own picker UI.

id.description: Stable identifier passed back to /select-provider and used in /v1/connect/{provider} routes.

category.description: Channel category — OTAs are listing marketplaces; PMSes are property management systems.

connectPattern.description: How the host is connected. `oauth`: provider-side consent screen. `credentials`: hosted form collects API keys. `activation`: push-only handshake (Vrbo). `claim`: connectivity-provider designation in the channel's Extranet (Booking.com).

status.description: Pickers should hide / disable `coming-soon` cards. `beta` cards are clickable but show a Beta pill.

logoUrl.description: Logo URL — Clearbit stand-in until self-hosted SVGs land.

description.example: OAuth consent — host approves access in one click.

aliases.description: Optional friendly aliases the picker's search box can match.

ConnectSession.description: A multi-channel Connect picker session. The `url` is the hosted picker page on connect.repull.dev — redirect the host to it, they pick a channel, and the picker takes them through the per-provider flow before redirecting back to the original `redirectUrl`.

state.description: Echoed back from the request body for SDK consumers that pass an opaque correlation token.

SelectProviderResponse.description: Returned by `POST /v1/connect/sessions/{sessionId}/select-provider`. The picker UI navigates the user to `nextUrl` to begin the per-provider handoff.

nextUrl.description: Where to send the user next — OAuth consent, credentials form, activation checklist, or claim form.

ConnectStatus.description: Connection status response for a single provider. When `connected` is false, all other fields except `provider` and `host` may be omitted, and `host` is null.

id.description: Repull-side connection ID. Stable across token refreshes.

externalAccountId.description: Provider-side account ID (e.g. the Airbnb host ID).

host.description: Host metadata, populated for Airbnb when the host row exists. Null for other providers (per-provider enrichment is incremental).

WebhookSubscription.description: A registered webhook endpoint. The `secret` field is only present in the response of `POST /v1/webhooks` and `POST /v1/webhooks/{id}/rotate-secret` (Stripe pattern — capture it then; it is masked everywhere else).

secret.description: Plaintext signing secret. Only returned by create + rotate. Capture and store securely.

WebhookDelivery.description: A single delivery attempt for a webhook event. The actual `WebhookEvent` envelope POSTed to the subscription URL is captured on `WebhookDeliveryDetail.payload` (this list view omits the body for size).

eventId.description: Stable across retries of the same logical event.

WebhookDeliveryDetail.description: Full request + response capture for one delivery attempt. `payload` is the exact `WebhookEvent` envelope that was (or would have been) POSTed to the subscription URL.

WebhookEventCatalog.description: Canonical catalog of every event the API can deliver, grouped by domain. Each entry includes a realistic `samplePayload` matching the discriminated `WebhookEvent` union — so SDKs can render docs and dashboards from this single source of truth.

flat.description: All events in a flat list (same entries as `domains[].events`, ungrouped).

samplePayload.description: Realistic example of the `data` payload an event of this `type` will deliver. Shape matches the matching variant in the `WebhookEvent` discriminated union.

WebhookEventType.description: Canonical event type identifier. Every webhook delivery declares one of these in its `type` field; SDKs key the discriminated `WebhookEvent` union on this value.

ReservationCreatedPayload.description: Payload for `reservation.created`. A new reservation arrived from any connected channel or direct booking.

ReservationUpdatedPayload.description: Payload for `reservation.updated`. Dates, guest count, status, or pricing changed on an existing reservation. The `changes` map carries `{ from, to }` deltas for each field that moved.

changes.description: Map of `field` → `{ from, to }` pairs describing what changed.

ReservationCancelledPayload.description: Payload for `reservation.cancelled`. A reservation was cancelled by the guest, host, or platform.

cancelledBy.description: Who initiated the cancellation (guest, host, platform).

ReservationMessageReceivedPayload.description: Payload for `reservation.message.received`. A new inbound message arrived on a reservation thread.

body.example: Hi! What time can we check in?

ListingCreatedPayload.description: Payload for `listing.created`. A new property was synced into Repull from a connected PMS or channel.

ListingUpdatedPayload.description: Payload for `listing.updated`. Listing content, amenities, photos, or status changed.

changes.description: Map of `field` → `{ from, to }` pairs describing what changed.

ListingDeletedPayload.description: Payload for `listing.deleted`. A property was removed from Repull or the upstream PMS.

CalendarUpdatedPayload.description: Payload for `calendar.updated`. Availability or pricing for a listing was updated.

ConnectionCreatedPayload.description: Payload for `connection.created`. An OAuth or API credential connection was completed by an end user.

provider.description: PMS or channel provider id (e.g. airbnb, booking, hostaway).

ConnectionDisconnectedPayload.description: Payload for `connection.disconnected`. A PMS or channel connection was revoked or expired.

connectionId.description: Stable connection identifier — alias of accountId for this event variant.

AiOperationCompletedPayload.description: Payload for `ai.operation.completed`. An async AI run (review response, message draft, pricing suggestion) finished.

type.description: AI operation kind — e.g. respond-to-guest, price-suggestion, review-response.

example.message: Free underground parking is included with your stay.

AiOperationFailedPayload.description: Payload for `ai.operation.failed`. An async AI run terminated with an error and will not be retried.

PaymentCompletedPayload.description: Payload for `payment.completed`. A guest payment was successfully captured.

PaymentRefundedPayload.description: Payload for `payment.refunded`. A previous payment was refunded in part or in full.

RepullPingPayload.description: Payload for `repull.ping`. A diagnostic delivery used by the dashboard to verify endpoint reachability.

message.example: Ping from Repull. If you can read this, your endpoint is reachable.

id.description: Stable event id — same across delivery retries of the same logical event.

WebhookEvent.description: The full event envelope POSTed to your webhook URL. Discriminated on `type` — narrow `event.data` by switching on `event.type`. Use the matching `*Event` variant directly if your SDK lacks discriminator support.

AirbnbListing.description: A Vanio listing paired with its Airbnb connection rows. The list endpoint groups every `listings_airbnb` row that points at the same Vanio `listingId` under a single `connections[]` array.

AirbnbConnection.description: An Airbnb-side connection record for a Vanio listing. The same property may appear under multiple connections if it has been linked from multiple Airbnb host accounts.

amenities.description: Present only when `?include=amenities` is passed. Sourced from `GET /v2/listings/:id/amenities` on Airbnb.

_errors.description: Per-expansion failures. Present only when an `?include=` upstream call failed for this connection (others may still succeed).

BookingProperty.description: A property registered in the Booking.com extranet for the connected hotel ID.

AirbnbReservation.description: An Airbnb reservation as returned by the channel API. Use `confirmationCode` to address it in Airbnb operations.

AirbnbReview.description: An Airbnb review (guest → host or host → guest).

CustomSchemaMappings.description: Field-mapping table. Keys are the output field names emitted in the response payload; values are simple expressions referenced against the source `native` payload (dot paths, basic arithmetic, string concatenation). Min 1 entry, max 50 entries. Each key must be <= 100 chars; each expression must be <= 500 chars and pass the safety check (no `eval`, no `function`, no `process`, etc.).

CustomSchema.description: A custom field-mapping schema owned by the workspace. Reshapes the `native` response into the workspace's preferred field names. Apply one per request via the `X-Schema: <name>` header on any read endpoint.

name.description: 3-100 lowercase chars, hyphens allowed (`^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{1,98}[a-z0-9]$`). Must be unique within the workspace. Cannot collide with reserved names (`calry`, `calry-v1`, `native`).

active.description: When `false`, requests carrying this schema name in `X-Schema` fall back to `native`.

CustomSchemaSummary.description: List-row shape returned by `GET /v1/schema/custom`. Same fields as `CustomSchema` minus heavy nested data — kept identical today; reserved as a separate schema so the list shape can shrink without breaking the detail call.

name.description: 3-100 lowercase chars + hyphens. Must be unique within the workspace and cannot collide with reserved names (`calry`, `calry-v1`, `native`).

description.description: Optional human-readable note shown in the dashboard.

CustomSchemaCreateResponse.description: Returned by `POST /v1/schema/custom` (201). Includes a `usage` hint telling the caller exactly which header value to set on subsequent requests.

CustomSchemaUpdate.description: Request body for `PATCH /v1/schema/custom/{id}`. All fields optional; omitted fields are left unchanged. `name` is intentionally NOT patchable — create a new schema and migrate consumers if you need to rename.

active.description: Toggle the schema on/off. When `false`, requests carrying this schema name in `X-Schema` fall back to `native`.

CustomSchemaListResponse.description: Returned by `GET /v1/schema/custom`. Returns every custom schema owned by the workspace.

Error.description: Standardized error envelope. Returned by EVERY 4xx/5xx response on this API. Required fields (`code`, `message`, `fix`, `docs_url`, `request_id`) are designed for LLM-driven self-recovery — an AI agent should be able to fix the underlying problem and retry without escalating to a human. Lead with `fix` and `docs_url` in your tooling; demote `support` (rare) to a last resort.

code.description: Stable machine-parseable error identifier. Match on this for retry logic. Codes are namespaced and never change meaning.

message.description: Human-readable cause. Echoes the offending value when relevant.
message.example: The check_in_after parameter must be an ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ). You sent: 'garbage'.

fix.description: Exact recovery steps. Surface this verbatim in your UI / agent reasoning trace — it is written to be actionable without further reading.
fix.example: Pass check_in_after as a string in ISO 8601 format. Example: ?check_in_after=2026-01-15

docs_url.description: Canonical write-up for this error code. URL pattern: `https://repull.dev/docs/errors/{code}`.

request_id.description: Opaque per-request id. Mirrors the `x-request-id` response header. Capture it before retrying so logs can be correlated.

field.description: Body field, query param, or path segment the error is about. Present when the error is parameter-specific.

value_received.description: Echo of the offending value (truncated to 200 chars). Useful for debugging — helps callers see what the server actually parsed.

valid_values.description: Allowed values when the error is enum-related (e.g. unknown `provider`, unknown `status`).

validParams.description: Sorted list of every query param this endpoint accepts. Present on `code: "unknown_params"` (HTTP 422) so SDK consumers can self-correct without reading docs.

endpoint.description: The endpoint path that produced the error. Present on `code: "unknown_params"` so consumers can match validation failures to the operation they invoked.

did_you_mean.description: Suggestion for typos and near-matches. Present when the server can guess the intent.

retry_after.description: Seconds the client should wait before retrying. Mirrors the `Retry-After` HTTP header. Present on rate-limit responses and on transient upstream failures that are safe to retry.

support.description: LAST-RESORT contact handle. Only set on errors that genuinely cannot be self-fixed (billing dispute, account-state corruption, OAuth partner intervention). Never fall back to support before trying `fix` and `docs_url`.

reference.description: Internal reference to quote when contacting support.

Pagination.description: Canonical cursor-based pagination envelope. Pass `nextCursor` back as `?cursor=` to fetch the next page; stop when `hasMore` is `false`. The cursor is opaque base64 — do not parse or construct it by hand.

nextCursor.description: Opaque base64 cursor — pass back as `?cursor=<value>`. `null` when there are no more pages.

total.description: Total rows matching the current filter (across all pages). Present when `?include_total=true` (the default on most endpoints). Omit `?include_total=false` to skip the COUNT(*) on very large workspaces.

ReservationListResponse.description: Cursor-paginated reservation list. Pass `pagination.nextCursor` back as `?cursor=` to fetch the next page; stop when `pagination.hasMore` is `false`. The `total` field is the count of rows matching the current filter (across all pages); pass `?include_total=false` to skip the COUNT(*) on very large workspaces.

GuestListResponse.description: Cursor-paginated guest list. Pass `pagination.nextCursor` back as `?cursor=` to fetch the next page.

ConversationListResponse.description: Cursor-paginated conversation list. Pass `pagination.nextCursor` back as `?cursor=` to fetch the next page.

MessageListResponse.description: Cursor-paginated message list. Pass `pagination.nextCursor` back as `?cursor=` to fetch the next page.

ReviewListResponse.description: Cursor-paginated review list. Pass `pagination.nextCursor` back as `?cursor=` to fetch the next page.

AirbnbReservationListResponse.description: Cursor-paginated Airbnb reservation list. Pass `pagination.next_cursor` back as `?cursor=` to fetch the next page; stop when `pagination.has_more` is `false`.

ListingCreateRequest.description: Inputs for `POST /v1/listings`. Provide enough address detail (street + city + lat/lng) for downstream Airbnb publish to work.

id.description: New listing ID — use for follow-up generate-content / publish calls

photos.description: Up to 8 reference photos. When present, Kimi K2 vision is used for grounded copy.

persist.description: Save the generated content to the listing (so subsequent publishes pick it up).

ListingPublishAirbnbRequest.description: Pass either `airbnbConnectionId` (update an already-mapped listing) or `hostId` (create a brand-new Airbnb listing under that host).

hostId.description: Airbnb host id (required for first-time creates)

force.description: Re-push every section, ignoring dirty-fields tracking

result.description: Channel-specific push result (sections pushed, errors, etc.)

ListingChannel.description: Per-platform connection for a listing — one row per channel the listing is published to.

externalId.description: ID in the platform (Airbnb listing id, Booking room id, etc.)

Listing.description: A vacation rental listing in your Repull workspace.

channels.description: Channels (Airbnb, Booking, VRBO, etc.) the listing is connected to.

ListingPricingRecommendation.description: A pricing recommendation for one date in the listing's calendar window.

currentPrice.description: Current calendar price (from Vanio listings_calendar_days) before applying the recommendation.

bookingProbability.description: Expected booking probability for the date at the recommended price.

factors.description: Free-form JSON of model factors (comp distance, event boost, weekend, demand, etc.).

listing.description: AI-derived base-price context for the listing.

dates.description: Dates the action applies to. Must match dates that have a `pending` recommendation; others are silently skipped.

applied.description: Number of recommendations applied (apply action only).

declined.description: Number of dates declined (decline action only).

ListingPricingStrategy.description: Strategy that constrains the Atlas pricing model for one listing.

mode.description: `recommend` surfaces suggestions; `auto` applies them on the next sync.

isDefault.description: true when no row exists yet and the response is server-side defaults.

ListingPricingStrategyInput.description: Same shape as `ListingPricingStrategy` minus the read-only fields. Send only fields you want to change.

BulkPricingItem.description: A single (listingId, dates) pair in a bulk pricing request. The action in the parent request body applies to every date in `dates` for this listing.

BulkPricingRequest.description: Body for `POST /v1/listings/pricing/bulk`. Apply or decline pending Atlas pricing recommendations across many listings in one call. Capped at 500 items per request — exceeding returns 422.

action.description: `apply` writes the recommended price to each listing's calendar and fans out to channels (Airbnb/Booking/VRBO). `decline` marks the recommendations as `declined` so they stop surfacing.

BulkPricingFailure.description: Per-item failure entry. Per-item failures DO NOT fail the whole batch — partial-success is the norm at this scale.

BulkPricingResponse.description: Response for `POST /v1/listings/pricing/bulk`. Per-item failures are returned granularly so the SDK consumer can retry just the bad entries.

processed.description: Total dates attempted across every item.

succeeded.description: Total dates that were successfully applied (or declined).

ListingPricingHistoryEntry.description: One date in the recommendation-vs-applied audit trail.

recommendedRate.description: The Atlas model's recommended price for the date.

appliedRate.description: Price actually written to the calendar. `null` when status is `pending` or `declined`. For now, when `status=applied` this equals `recommended_rate` because the apply path writes the recommendation verbatim.

status.description: `overridden` is reserved for a future signal — it never appears today.

recommendationFactors.description: Raw model factors (comp distance, event boost, weekend, demand, etc.).

ListingPricingHistoryResponse.description: Cursor-paginated audit trail. Pagination is keyset on `date ASC` — stable across concurrent writes.

ListingComp.description: A single competitor listing in a comp set, sorted closest-first. Includes per-day rate/availability for the requested calendar window.

distanceKm.description: Haversine distance from the source listing in km, rounded to 3 decimals.

currentNightlyRate.description: Latest snapshot ADR — fallback to render when the calendar window is empty.

nightly.description: Per-day rate + availability for the requested window. May be empty if Atlas hasn't snapshotted the comp recently.

externalUrl.description: Link to the listing on its source platform when one is available.

ListingCompsResponse.description: Returned by `GET /v1/listings/{id}/comps`. Comps without coordinates are excluded — there's no way to rank them by distance. May include a `warning` field when the source listing itself has no coordinates.

warning.description: Present (and `data` empty) when the source listing has no coordinates.

ListingSegment.description: One Atlas DNA segment (e.g. `upscale-modern-2br`) with share + ADR aggregates across the scoped comp set or market.

sharePct.description: Percent of analyzed comps in the scope that fall in this segment.

myListingMatch.description: True when the source listing's `ai_segment` matches this segment.

ListingSegmentRecommendation.description: Structural observation about the segment landscape — not LLM-generated.

kind.description: Stable identifier for the recommendation kind. SDKs can switch on this safely.

ListingSegmentsResponse.description: Returned by `GET /v1/listings/{id}/segments`. Honest about DNA coverage — when no comps in the scope have been DNA-scored, returns `totalCompsAnalyzed: 0` plus a `low_dna_coverage` recommendation rather than fabricated data.

scope.description: When `level=comp_set` carries `radiusKm`; when `level=market` carries `city`. May be empty when neither could be resolved.

mySegment.description: The source listing's own `ai_segment` (or null if not yet scored).

totalCompsAnalyzed.description: Number of comps in scope that have a DNA score. `0` is a coverage signal, not an error.

BookingPricingRateUpdateRestrictions.description: Optional length-of-stay / availability restrictions for one rate update.

BookingPricingRateUpdate.description: A single (room, rate-plan, date-range) update pushed to Booking.com via the rates API.

roomId.description: Booking.com room ID for the rate plan. Comes from `listings_booking_rooms` mapping.

BookingPricingUpdateRequest.description: Body for `PUT /v1/channels/booking/listings/{id}/pricing`. Pricing on Booking is per-room/per-rate-plan, so `room_id` + `rate_id` are required on every update.

pushed.description: Number of updates Booking.com accepted as `success`. Falls back to total update count when Booking omits per-update status on full success.

errors.description: Per-update failure rows from Booking — shape mirrors the Booking rates API response.

raw.description: Verbatim Booking response envelope for debugging.

BookingPricingResponse.description: Returned by `GET /v1/channels/booking/listings/{id}/pricing`. Mirrors Booking's `getRoomRateAvailability` response with `hotelId` and `listingId` echoed back for SDK consumers.

MarketSummary.description: Per-city KPIs combining the customer's own listings with Atlas comp aggregates.

totalListings.description: Atlas-tracked active comps in this city.

propertyTypes.description: Distinct property types Atlas has seen in this city.

MarketMyListing.description: Lightweight customer listing entry for map rendering.

MarketBrowseFeatured.description: Curated featured market entry on the discovery summary. Enriched with `subscribed` so the dashboard can render "Already unlocked" pills without an extra round-trip.

listings.description: Atlas-tracked active comps in this city.

avgAdr.description: Atlas-aggregated avg nightly rate (mixed currency, dominated by the country base).

count.description: Number of Atlas-tracked cities in this country.

listings.description: Atlas-tracked active comps in this city.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency derived from the country code.

MarketsOverviewResponse.description: Overview of every market the customer operates in, plus auxiliary discovery slices. Wraps the canonical `{ data, pagination }` envelope around the per-city KPI list (`data`) so SDK consumers see the same shape they get from every other list endpoint. Auxiliary fields (`totals`, `myListings`, `browse`, `freeMarket`, `subscriptions`, `tier`) are returned as siblings because they are NOT paginated. The overview returns every market in one shot — `nextCursor` is always `null` and `hasMore` is always `false`.

data.description: Per-city KPIs for every market the customer operates in.

freeMarket.description: City auto-assigned as the customer's free market (largest by listing count). Null for customers with no listings.

subscriptions.description: Active per-market unlocks vs the tier quota.

browse.description: Lightweight discovery summary. Use `/v1/markets/browse` for the full paginated catalog.

featured.description: Top ~50 markets by listing volume the customer doesn't already operate in.

totalAvailable.description: Total Atlas-tracked cities in the catalog.

MarketDetailResponse.description: Detailed view for a single city. Several sub-objects are passed through verbatim from upstream — keys mirror the underlying SQL aggregations.

BookingVerifyHotelRequest.description: Body for `POST /v1/connect/booking/verify`. Manual-paste fallback that closes a Booking.com Connect session after the customer completes Stage 1 designation in the Extranet.

sessionId.description: The Connect session ID returned by `createConnectSession`. Acts as the capability token — no API key required.

hotelId.description: Booking.com hotel ID the customer pasted. 6+ digits.

BookingVerifyHotelResponse.description: Successful verify response. The session is transitioned to `awaiting_room_mapping` and a `pms_connections` row is upserted.

connectionId.description: Repull-side `pms_connections.id` for the linked Booking account.

BookingConnectRoom.description: A Booking.com room imported from the claimed hotel. The customer maps each room to one of their Repull listings via `mapConnectBookingRooms`.

roomId.description: Repull-side `listings_booking_rooms.id`. Pass this back in the mapping submission.

numberOfRooms.description: Number of inventory units of this room type at the hotel.

currentListingId.description: Currently mapped Repull listing ID, or null if not yet mapped.

BookingConnectListingOption.description: A Repull listing the customer can map a Booking room to. Mirrors the minimal shape needed for a select dropdown.

BookingConnectRoomsResponse.description: Returned by `GET /v1/connect/booking/rooms`. The hosted picker page polls this every ~2s while the room import runs server-side; once `status` is `ready` it renders the mapping UI.

status.description: `importing` — listings_booking row exists but rooms not yet imported. `ready` — rooms imported, awaiting mapping. `completed` — session already finished.

BookingRoomMapping.description: A single room→listing assignment. Pass `listingId: null` to explicitly UNMAP a room (e.g. "skip this room for now") — this also removes the corresponding `listing_platform_links` row.

listingId.description: Repull listing to bind to this room. `null` to unmap.

MapConnectBookingRoomsRequest.description: Body for `POST /v1/connect/booking/map-rooms`. Submits all room→listing assignments in one transaction; on success the Connect session is marked `completed`.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: Max items per page (cap is 100; over-cap returns 422).

cursor.name: cursor
cursor.description: Opaque base64 cursor returned in the previous response's `pagination.nextCursor`. Omit to fetch the first page.

provider.name: provider
provider.description: PMS provider slug (e.g., hostaway, guesty, ownerrez)

XSchemaHeader.name: X-Schema
XSchemaHeader.description: Apply a custom or built-in schema to transform the response. Built-in: `native` (default), `calry`, `calry-v1`. Custom: any schema name created via `POST /v1/schema/custom`. Unknown / inactive schema names fall back to `native`.

IncludeTotal.name: include_total
IncludeTotal.description: When `true` (default), the response's `pagination.total` carries the count of rows matching the current filter, across all pages. Pass `false` to skip the count for very large workspaces where the per-page COUNT(*) cost matters.

BadRequest.description: Malformed request — invalid JSON, missing required field, wrong content-type, or a parameter that fundamentally cannot be parsed.

error.message: Request body is not valid JSON.
error.fix: Send the body with `Content-Type: application/json` and a JSON document. The first parse error was at character 17 (unexpected `,`).

Unauthorized.description: No API key on the request, or the key is unknown / revoked.

error.message: No API key on the request. Pass it via the `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header (or the `x-api-key` header).
error.fix: Add `Authorization: Bearer sk_test_<your-key>` to the request. Sandbox keys start with `sk_test_`, production with `sk_live_`. Get a key at https://repull.dev/dashboard/api-keys.

error.message: The API key on this request is unknown or has been revoked.
error.fix: Confirm the key is copied exactly from https://repull.dev/dashboard/api-keys (no leading/trailing whitespace) and that the prefix matches the environment you intend to hit (`sk_test_` for sandbox, `sk_live_` for production). If the key was rotated, update the consumer with the new value.

Forbidden.description: The API key is valid but lacks access to this resource or product.

missingEntitlement.summary: Workspace not entitled to the requested product

error.fix: Subscribe to the markets product at https://repull.dev/dashboard/billing — it activates immediately and unlocks every `/v1/markets/*` and `/v1/atlas/*` endpoint.

NotFound.description: The resource id is well-formed but no matching row exists in this workspace.

notFound.summary: Resource id not found in this workspace

error.message: Reservation 999999 was not found in this workspace.
error.fix: Verify the id exists in this workspace via `GET /v1/reservations`. Ids from one workspace are not valid in another — switch API keys if you intended a different workspace.

Conflict.description: The request conflicts with the current resource state (already-exists, terminal, mid-flight).

terminalSession.summary: Session already in a terminal state

error.message: Session is in terminal state: succeeded.
error.fix: Terminal sessions cannot be reused. Start a new session via `POST /v1/connect/{provider}` to retry.

UnprocessableEntity.description: Request is well-formed but a parameter value is out of range or fails validation.

badIsoDate.summary: Parameter is not a valid ISO 8601 date

error.message: The check_in_after parameter must be an ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ). You sent: 'garbage'.
error.fix: Pass check_in_after as a string in ISO 8601 format. Example: ?check_in_after=2026-01-15

error.message: limit=500 exceeds the per-page cap of 100 for this endpoint.
error.fix: Lower `limit` to 100 or below and walk pages with `?cursor=<pagination.nextCursor>`.

unknownParams.summary: Query parameter is not in the endpoint's allowlist

error.fix: Did you mean 'q'? Valid params: cursor, has_reservation, include_total, limit, listing_id, q.

TooManyRequests.description: Quota exceeded. Wait `retry_after` seconds (or until `X-RateLimit-Reset`) before retrying.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Total requests allowed in the current window.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Requests remaining in the current window.

error.message: You have exceeded your monthly request quota for the 'free' tier (10000 / 10000). Quota resets 2026-06-01T00:00:00.000Z.
error.fix: Wait 2592000s for the new billing month, or upgrade your plan at https://repull.dev/dashboard/billing to lift the cap immediately.

InternalError.description: Unexpected server-side failure. Always retryable with backoff.

error.fix: Retry the request with exponential backoff. If the same request_id keeps failing, capture it (and the timestamp) for diagnosis.

get.description: Liveness probe. Returns `{ status: "ok", version }` when the API process is running. No auth required. Suitable for uptime monitors, load-balancer health checks, and SDK self-tests.

Cursor-paginated list of properties for the authenticated workspace. Walk pages with `?cursor=<pagination.nextCursor>`; stop when `pagination.hasMore` is `false`. Cursor is opaque base64 — do not parse it.

Filters: `q` (substring on name/street/city), `status` (active|inactive|all), `lifecycle_status` (exact match on the listing's lifecycle state).

**Breaking change:** `?offset=` is no longer accepted. Requests passing it return 422 with a `did_you_mean: 'cursor'` hint. Other unknown params (e.g. `?search=` or `?propertyId=`) are also rejected with 422 — no silent unfiltered results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size (max 100). Requests over the cap return 422.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned in the previous response's `pagination.nextCursor`. Omit to fetch the first page.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring search on name, street, or city.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status. Default returns active only; pass `inactive` to invert or `all` to include both.

parameters.name: lifecycle_status
parameters.description: Filter by lifecycle status (e.g. `live`, `draft`, `archived`). Pass `all` to disable the filter.

get.description: Fetch a single property by Repull id. Property ids are workspace-scoped — an id from one workspace is not valid in another. 404 means the id does not exist OR belongs to a different workspace.

Cursor-paginated list of reservations across all connected PMS platforms. Filter by platform, status, listing, or check-in date range.

**Pagination:** Walk pages with `?cursor=` — pass `pagination.nextCursor` from one response back as `?cursor=` on the next request. Stop when `pagination.hasMore` is `false`. `limit` defaults to 50, max 100; requesting more returns 422 (no silent truncation).

**Breaking change:** `?offset=` is no longer accepted. Requests passing it return 422 with a `did_you_mean: 'cursor'` hint.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size (max 100). Requests over the cap return 422.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned in the previous response's `pagination.nextCursor`. Omit to fetch the first page.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Filter by booking platform

parameters.name: listingId
parameters.description: Filter to a single listing

parameters.name: check_in_after
parameters.description: Check-in date >= this value

parameters.name: check_in_before
parameters.description: Check-in date <= this value

parameters.name: checkInFrom
parameters.description: Deprecated alias for `check_in_after`.

parameters.name: checkInTo
parameters.description: Deprecated alias for `check_in_before`.

post.description: Create a reservation in the source PMS. Required fields depend on the connected provider (e.g. Airbnb requires guest email; Booking.com requires hotel id). Validation errors return 422 with the offending `field` populated.

get.description: Returns the full record for a single reservation, scoped to the authenticated workspace. Response shape is identical to a single row in `GET /v1/reservations` so SDK consumers can use the same type for both. Returns **404** if the id does not exist OR belongs to a different workspace — the API never differentiates the two so caller can't enumerate other workspaces' ids.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Internal Repull reservation ID.

patch.description: Patch reservation fields (dates, status, special requests). Only fields included in the body are modified. Use the cancel endpoint for cancellations — DELETE handles cancellation but not partial updates.

delete.description: Cancel an existing reservation. Cancellation rules vary by provider — Airbnb host-cancellations carry penalties; Booking.com cancellations apply the per-rate-plan policy. Once 200 is returned, the upstream PMS state is committed.

get.description: Returns day-by-day availability, pricing, and minimum stay for a property.

put.description: Update pricing, availability, and minimum stay for specific dates.

get.description:
Cursor-paginated list of guests in the workspace. Walks `guests.id ASC` keyset for constant per-page cost regardless of how many guests the customer has. Use `pagination.nextCursor` from one response as the `cursor` query param of the next request.

Filters: `q` (substring on name/email/phone), `has_reservation` (`true`|`false`), `listing_id` (restrict to guests with at least one reservation on that listing).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned in the previous response's `pagination.nextCursor`. Omit to fetch the first page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max items per page. Hard cap is 100.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring search on name, email, or phone.

parameters.name: has_reservation
parameters.description: Restrict to guests that do (`true`) or do not (`false`) have any reservation on file.

parameters.name: listingId
parameters.description: Restrict to guests with at least one reservation on the given internal Repull listing id.

get.description: Returns the full guest profile — base list-row fields plus contacts, flags, notes, risk metadata, and reservation aggregates. Aggregates main vanio's `GuestService.getGuestProfile()` into the public Repull shape so SDK consumers don't have to learn the internal schema.

Cursor-paginated list of message threads owned by the workspace. Backed by main vanio's `/api/threads/list` which keyset-paginates against `(last_message_at, id)` for constant per-page cost. Use `pagination.nextCursor` from one response as the `cursor` query param of the next request.

Filters: `platform` (`airbnb`|`booking`|`vrbo`|`website`|`email`), `status` (`open`|`archived` — `archived` is a stable no-op until the bit lands on `message_threads`).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned in the previous response's `pagination.nextCursor`. Omit to fetch the first page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max items per page. Hard cap is 100.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Restrict to threads on a single channel.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by archive status. `archived` currently always returns an empty page — kept for forward-compat.

get.description: Returns one thread (the same shape as the list-row `Conversation`) plus expanded `host` (from `airbnb_hosts` for the thread's `host_id`) and `guest` (resolved via the thread's `reservation_id`, with up to 50 contacts) blocks.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Internal Repull thread id.

get.description: Cursor-paginated messages within one thread. Defaults to newest-first (`?order=desc`); pass `?order=asc` for chronological replay. Use `pagination.nextCursor` from one response as the `cursor` query param of the next request.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Internal Repull thread id.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned in the previous response's `pagination.nextCursor`.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: `desc` (default) returns newest first. `asc` returns chronological replay.

get.description:
Cursor-paginated guest + host review stream for the workspace. Backed by main vanio's unified `reviews` table (populated by per-channel backfill crons), so this surface returns the complete cross-channel history — separate from `/v1/channels/airbnb/reviews` which hits Airbnb live.

Filters: `platform` (`airbnb`|`booking`|`vrbo`), `listing_id` (internal Repull listing id), `rating_min` / `rating_max` (inclusive bounds, 0..5), `status` (`responded`|`unanswered`|`all`), `reviewer_role` (`guest` (default) | `host` | `all`).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned in the previous response's `pagination.nextCursor`.

parameters.name: listingId
parameters.description: Restrict to one internal Repull listing.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: `responded` — host has replied. `unanswered` — host has not replied. `all` — no filter.

parameters.name: reviewerRole
parameters.description: `guest` (default) — reviews written by guests about the host/property. `host` — reviews written by the host about guests. `all` — both.

get.description: Returns one review (the bare `Review` object — NOT wrapped in `{ data: ... }`). Scoped to the authenticated workspace via the listings join — reviews that don't belong to the workspace return 404 (we don't differentiate to avoid leaking other customers' ids).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Internal Repull review id.

get.description: Returns all active connections to PMS and OTA platforms.

post.summary: Create a multi-channel Connect picker session
post.description:
Mints a session that lands the user on the channel picker at `connect.repull.dev/{sessionId}` instead of jumping straight to a single provider. The user picks a channel from the registry, the picker page POSTs `selectConnectProvider` to bind the choice, and the per-provider flow takes over.

Use this when you want one entry point for all 13 channels. Use `POST /v1/connect/{provider}` instead when your UI already knows which channel to connect.

redirectUrl.description: Where to send the user after they finish (or cancel). Status query params are appended.

state.description: Opaque pass-through correlation token. Echoed back in the response.

allowedProviders.description: Optional whitelist of provider IDs the picker should expose. Omit to show every channel in the registry.

get.description: Returns the public registry of every channel the picker supports. No customer-specific data — display metadata only. Cached for 5 minutes at the edge.

post.summary: Bind a picker session to a provider
post.description:
Called by the hosted picker page once the user clicks a channel card. Validates the provider exists and is permitted by the session's `allowed_providers` whitelist (if any), then returns the next-step URL the picker should navigate to.

No API key required — the session ID is the capability token. The session must still be pending and unexpired.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The picker session ID returned by `createConnectSession`.

get.description: Returns the current connection status for a provider, including host metadata (display name + avatar) for Airbnb so clients can render an account-level confirmation UI.

post.description:
Establish a connection to a PMS or OTA platform. Credentials vary by provider — see docs for each provider.

Airbnb-specific: pass `redirectUrl` (where to send the user after consent) and optionally `accessType` (`read_only` for calendar-only OAuth scopes, or `full_access` — the default — for full host scopes). The response returns a hosted `url` to redirect the user to.

schema.description: Provider-specific credentials (apiKey, clientId/clientSecret, etc.) or OAuth init params for Airbnb.

redirectUrl.description: Airbnb only — where to redirect the user after the OAuth flow completes.

accessType.description: Airbnb only — selects the OAuth scope set. 'read_only' grants calendar-only access; 'full_access' grants full host scopes (default).

delete.description: Disconnect a PMS or OTA from this workspace. Revokes the OAuth token (where applicable), purges credentials, and stops all sync jobs. Resources synced from the provider remain queryable but become read-only and stop receiving updates.

post.summary: Verify a Booking.com hotel ID for a Connect session
post.description:
Manual-paste fallback that closes the Booking.com claim flow. Call this after the customer completes Stage 1 designation in their Booking Extranet (ticking FantasticStay/Repull as their connectivity provider) and pastes their Hotel ID into the hosted picker.

Validates the hotel against Booking's property API, persists the `pms_connections` row, kicks off the room import, and transitions the Connect session to `awaiting_room_mapping`.

No API key required — the `sessionId` is the capability token. Sessions in any terminal state are rejected.

410.description: Session has expired — start a new one

412.description: Stage 1 designation not yet complete in the Extranet

503.description: Booking.com client credentials not configured on this deployment

get.summary: List Booking.com rooms imported for a Connect session
get.description:
Returns the rooms imported from the Booking.com hotel claimed in this Connect session, plus the customer's listing options for the mapping dropdowns. Hosted-picker pages poll this endpoint every ~2s after `verifyBookingHotel` succeeds; once rooms appear the page transitions to the mapping UI.

No API key required — the `sessionId` query param is the capability token.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The Connect session ID returned by `createConnectSession`.

425.description: Hotel claim not yet persisted — retry shortly

post.summary: Submit room→listing mappings for a Booking.com Connect session
post.description:
Submits the customer's room→listing mapping choices in one transaction. For each mapping, updates `listings_booking_rooms.listing_id` and replaces the corresponding `listing_platform_links` row. Pass `listingId: null` to explicitly unmap a room.

On success the Connect session is marked `completed` and the hosted picker page emits a `repull:connect:completed` postMessage to the embedding window.

No API key required — the `sessionId` in the body is the capability token. Each mapping's `roomId` must belong to the customer's claimed hotel; mismatched IDs are rejected with 403.

200.description: All mappings applied; session marked completed

403.description: A room ID does not belong to this customer's claimed hotel

425.description: No Booking.com connection found for this customer

get.description: List every webhook subscription registered for this workspace. Each row includes the destination URL, subscribed event types, and the most recent delivery summary.

post.description: Register a new endpoint. Returns the plaintext signing secret ONCE — capture it from the response and store it securely. After this call the secret is masked everywhere; mint a new one with `POST /v1/webhooks/{id}/rotate-secret` if you lose it. See `GET /v1/webhooks/event-types` for the full list of subscribable events.

get.description: The canonical catalog of every event the API can deliver, grouped by domain, with realistic sample payloads.
Fetch a single webhook subscription by id. Use the `deliveries` sub-resource to list recent attempts, and `ping` to send a test event.

patch.description: Update url, description, events, or status (active|paused). Re-enabling clears `consecutive_failures` and `disabled_at`.

delete.description: Delete a webhook subscription. In-flight deliveries already on the queue are still attempted; new events stop firing immediately.

post.description: Mints a fresh signing secret and returns the plaintext ONCE. After this response the secret is masked everywhere — capture and store it now.
Fires a synthetic `repull.ping` at the subscription URL and returns the full delivery cycle inline. Used by dashboards and health-check probes.

200.description: Ping delivered (success or failure — check the response fields)

post.summary: Send test event of a specific type
post.description: Delivers a realistic fixture payload of the requested event type to the subscription URL.

get.description: Cursor-paginated history of every delivery attempt for this subscription. Walk pages with `?cursor=<pagination.nextCursor>`; stop when `pagination.hasMore` is `false`. The cursor is opaque base64 — do not parse it.

schema.description: Opaque cursor returned in the previous response's `pagination.nextCursor`. Omit to fetch the first page.

get.description: Full request + response capture for one delivery attempt.

post.description: Re-sends the original payload (same eventId, fresh deliveryId, attempt + 1).

post.summary: [Legacy] Send test webhook to a URL
post.description: Deprecated: prefer creating a subscription then calling `POST /v1/webhooks/{id}/ping`. Kept for back-compat.

get.description:
List every Airbnb listing this workspace has access to via the connected Airbnb account. Default response is a fast DB read pairing each Vanio listing with its `listings_airbnb` connection rows.

Pass `?include=amenities` to enrich each connection with its current Airbnb amenity set (one extra upstream call per unique Airbnb id, fanned out in parallel). Per-connection failures surface in `_errors.amenities` rather than failing the whole request.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated expansions. Currently supported: `amenities` (adds `amenities` and `accessibility_amenities` arrays to each connection). Each expansion adds one upstream Airbnb call per unique listing id.

post.description: Create a new Airbnb listing or push an existing Repull listing to Airbnb. Requires a connected Airbnb account. Returns the created listing id; publishing happens via the listing-action endpoint.

get.description: Fetch all Airbnb connection rows for a single Vanio listing id. A property may be linked from multiple Airbnb hosts — every match is returned. Pass `?include=amenities` to enrich each row with its current Airbnb amenities.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated expansions. Currently supported: `amenities`.

post.description: Apply a state action to an Airbnb listing — `push` (sync local changes upstream), `publish` (make publicly bookable), `unlist` (hide), or `delete` (permanent). Each action has different reversibility — see docs.

get.description: Read the current pricing config (base price, weekend uplift, length-of-stay discounts, smart-pricing bounds) for an Airbnb listing.

put.description: Push pricing changes to Airbnb. The full pricing object is replaced — to patch a single field, GET first, mutate locally, then PUT the whole object.

get.description: Read the per-day availability calendar for an Airbnb listing. Returns one row per day including price overrides, min-stay, and blocked status.

put.description: Push per-day availability + pricing overrides to Airbnb. Accepts a sparse map (date → fields) — only included dates are updated.

get.description: List photos attached to an Airbnb listing in display order. Returns the public CDN URL plus Airbnb-side metadata (id, caption, room).

post.description: Upload one or more photos to an Airbnb listing. Accepts public image URLs (Airbnb fetches them) — direct binary upload is not supported on this endpoint.

get.description: List Airbnb message threads (one per guest conversation). Cursor-paginated. Each thread includes a preview of the latest message.
Fetch the full message log for an Airbnb thread, ordered oldest-to-newest. Walk pages with `?cursor=` until `pagination.hasMore` is `false`.

post.description: Send a message in an Airbnb thread as the host. Airbnb enforces content rules (no off-platform contact info, no external URLs) — violating messages are rejected upstream and surface as `airbnb_error`.

get.description:
Cursor-paginated list of reservations sourced directly from Airbnb. Use this when you need Airbnb-specific fields (guest payout split, cancellation policy snapshot) that the unified `/v1/reservations` endpoint flattens away.

Walk pages with `?cursor=<pagination.next_cursor>` until `pagination.has_more` is `false`. The cursor is opaque — never construct or parse it client-side.

When `status` is omitted, all statuses are returned (Airbnb defaults to `accepted` only on its own surface, but this endpoint normalises to "all"). Pass `?status=accepted` to scope.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned by the previous response's `pagination.next_cursor`. Omit to fetch the first page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max items per page. Hard cap is 100.

parameters.name: listing_id
parameters.description: Filter to one Airbnb listing id (numeric string).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by reservation status. Omit to receive all statuses.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) lower bound on Airbnb's date range filter.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) upper bound on Airbnb's date range filter.

parameters.name: include_total
parameters.description: Whether to include `pagination.total`. Always populated when Airbnb returns a total count (effectively always); accepted for shape symmetry with the rest of the API.

get.description: Fetch a single Airbnb reservation by Airbnb confirmation code (e.g. `HMABCDEF12`).

post.description: Apply a state action to an Airbnb reservation — `accept` / `decline` (for inquiries and reservation requests), `cancel` (host cancellation, carries penalties), `pre-approve` (for inquiries).

get.description: List reviews left by guests on Airbnb listings in this workspace. Includes both reviews of the host and reviews of the guest (where the host has not yet submitted theirs).

post.description: Legacy action-based shape. Body `{ action: "respond"|"submit", reviewId, response?, review? }`. Kept for backwards compatibility — prefer `PUT /v1/channels/airbnb/reviews/{id}` (edit) and `POST /v1/channels/airbnb/reviews/{id}/respond` (reply) for new integrations.

put.description:
Edit a host-side review for an Airbnb stay. Airbnb collapses POST + PUT into the same upstream call (`PUT /v2/listing_reviews/{id}`), so this endpoint covers both initial submit and subsequent edits while the review window is open.

Body is a partial `AirbnbReview` — pass the fields you want to change (rating, public review, private feedback, category ratings).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Airbnb review id (`HRabc123` style).

post.description: Post a public host response to a guest review. Airbnb allows one response per review — repeated POSTs return 409. Response text is capped at 1000 characters.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Airbnb review id.

response.description: Public response text. Capped at 1000 characters.

post.description: Push all property data to Airbnb in one call.

get.description: Cursor-paginated list of listings owned by the authenticated workspace. Use `pagination.nextCursor` from one response as the `cursor` query param of the next request to walk the full set. Filters: `q` (substring on name/street/city), `status`, `channel`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned in the previous response's `pagination.nextCursor`. Omit to fetch the first page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max items per page. Hard cap is 100.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring search on name, street, or city.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by listing status.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: Restrict to listings published on the given channel (`airbnb`, `booking`, `vrbo`, etc.). Joins through `listing_platform_links` and matches active links only.

post.description: Create a new vacation-rental listing under the authenticated workspace. The listing is stored in the canonical Vanio listings tables and can be published to multiple channels (Airbnb, Booking.com) via the publish endpoints.

get.description: Fetch a single listing by id. Returns the same shape as one element of the `GET /v1/listings` response, so you can bind the result to the same model. Cross-tenant access (a listing that belongs to a different workspace) returns 404 — never 403, never reveals the listing's existence.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Repull listing id

post.description: Generate guest-facing copy (title, summary, description, amenities, etc.) for a listing using Kimi K2. When `photos` are provided the vision model is used for photo-grounded copy. Persists into the listing by default.

502.description: AI provider failed or returned non-JSON

post.description: Push a Repull listing to Airbnb. Pass `airbnbConnectionId` to update an already-mapped Airbnb listing, or `hostId` to create a brand-new Airbnb listing under that host.
Push a Repull listing to Booking.com. The listing must already be mapped to a Booking property + room (created via the Booking-claim Connect flow).

get.description: Returns one row per platform the listing has been pushed/pulled to, with last push timestamp and any dirty fields not yet synced.
List Booking.com hotels claimed by this workspace. Each row includes the Booking-side hotel id and the connected room types.

post.description: Onboard a new Booking.com hotel via the OAuth Connect flow. Returns the hotel id once Stage-1 designation completes in the Extranet.

put.description: Push availability + rate changes to Booking.com's OTA system. Accepts the standard OTA rate message — see Booking's OTA docs for the field shape. Errors from upstream surface as `booking_error`.

get.description: Fetch the current content (descriptions, amenities, photos) for a Booking.com property. Used to round-trip edits through Repull.

post.description: Push content changes (descriptions, amenities, photos) to Booking.com. Booking enforces editorial review on text fields — changes appear after their content moderation queue clears.

get.description: List Booking.com guest conversations. Cursor-paginated. Use the messaging POST to send a reply.

post.description: Send a message in a Booking.com conversation as the host. Booking enforces content rules similar to Airbnb.
Trigger a full bulk sync of properties + availability + rates to Booking.com. Runs async — returns 202 with a job id; poll `/v1/sync/jobs/{id}` for status.

get.description: List guest reviews for a Booking.com property. Pass `property_id` (the Booking.com hotel id) as a query param — required.

parameters.name: property_id
parameters.description: Booking.com hotel/property id.

post.description:
Post a public host reply to a guest review on Booking.com. Booking allows one host reply per review — repeated POSTs are rejected by upstream.

Booking.com does NOT support host-authored reviews of guests via the API (platform-level limitation), so this endpoint is reply-only.

get.summary: Get Booking.com pricing for a listing
get.description:
Resolves the Vanio listing ID to its Booking.com `hotel_id` (via the `listings_booking` mapping owned by the authenticated workspace), then proxies Booking's `getRoomRateAvailability` for the requested window. Pricing on Booking is per-room/per-rate-plan, so `room_id` and `room_level` flow through query params unchanged.

Mirrors the per-channel `/listings/{id}/pricing` shape used by Airbnb so SDK consumers can carry a Vanio listing ID across channels.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Vanio listing ID — resolved to a Booking.com hotel ID via the workspace mapping.

parameters.name: room_level
parameters.description: When true, returns room-level (vs rate-plan-level) availability.

put.summary: Update Booking.com pricing for a listing
put.description: Pushes one or more rate updates to Booking.com via `updateRates`. Each update needs `roomId` + `rateId` + `dateRange` + `price` + `currency`. Field-level validation runs up front so callers don't have to parse Booking's XML error envelope to discover a missing `roomId`.

get.description: List VRBO listings this workspace owns. VRBO is agency-model — Repull reads listings via the public iCal/HTTP feeds.

VRBO uses the agency model — VRBO PULLS rates from `/api/webhooks/vrbo/listings-xml/rates/{listing}/{unit}` rather than accepting a push API. This endpoint is declared for symmetry with the other channel-pricing routes but currently returns **501 Not Implemented** with a pointer at the public rate URL VRBO consumes. Use `GET /v1/listings/{id}/calendar` (once wired) to inspect the underlying source-of-truth.

When the listings-XML rate-builder is ported into this repo, this endpoint will return the parsed rates VRBO sees.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Vanio listing ID — resolved to a VRBO listing/unit via the workspace mapping.

501.description: Not implemented — VRBO is agency-model. Response includes the public rate URL VRBO fetches.

put.summary: Update VRBO pricing (501 — no push API exists)
put.description: VRBO has no rate-push API. To change what VRBO sees, update the underlying Vanio calendar/pricing-settings (e.g. `PUT /v1/listings/{id}/calendar` once wired) — VRBO will pick up the change on its next pull. This endpoint always returns **501** rather than fake-stubbing a successful push the SDK would silently swallow.

501.description: Not implemented — VRBO accepts no rate pushes. Update the calendar instead.

get.description: List VRBO reservations sourced from the public booking feed. Lag is typically 5-10 minutes vs. Airbnb / Booking.com.
List Plumguide listings this workspace has access to. Plumguide is approval-based — listings appear once Plumguide has accepted them.
Read the per-day availability calendar for a Plumguide listing. Returns the same row shape as Airbnb availability for SDK convenience.

put.description: Push per-day availability changes to Plumguide. Plumguide accepts only the next 24 months — dates beyond that are silently ignored.

get.description: Read the current pricing for a Plumguide listing (base price, currency, weekend uplift).

put.description: Push pricing changes to Plumguide. Plumguide rounds all prices to whole units of the listing currency — sub-unit precision is silently truncated.

get.description: Returns date-by-date pricing recommendations for a listing's upcoming calendar window, plus the listing's base-price context and a 5km comp summary. Recommendations come from the Atlas pricing model — pre-computed nightly and stored in `pricing_recommendations`. Use POST to apply or decline pending recommendations.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Listing ID

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Inclusive start of the calendar window. Defaults to today.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Inclusive end of the calendar window. Defaults to today + 90 days.

post.description: Apply: writes the recommended price to the listing's calendar for the given dates and triggers the platform fan-out (Airbnb / Booking.com / VRBO). Decline: marks the recommendation as `declined` so it stops surfacing — the model can re-recommend on the next training cycle.

get.description: Returns the strategy that constrains how the Atlas pricing model behaves for this listing. If no strategy row exists yet, returns sane defaults flagged with `isDefault: true`.

put.description: Upserts the strategy on `(listing_id, customer_id)` — repeated PUTs are idempotent. Send only the fields you want to change; omitted fields take server-side defaults.

post.summary: Bulk apply or decline pricing recommendations
post.description:
Apply or decline pending Atlas pricing recommendations across many listings in one call. Built for power users with hundreds of listings who would otherwise need 500 sequential single-listing POSTs.

- `items` is capped at 500 entries per request — exceeding returns 422.
- Per-item failures (stale listing IDs, no pending recs, channel auth blips) DO NOT fail the whole batch — partial success is the norm at this scale and the granular `failed[]` array lets the SDK retry just the bad entries.
- Tier-limit accounting: this endpoint counts as **1 API call** regardless of how many items the body contains.

Apply path writes the recommended price to each listing's calendar via the calendar service (which fans out to Airbnb/Booking/VRBO) then marks the Atlas recommendation `applied`. Decline path is Atlas-only — fast.

200.description: Bulk action processed (check `failed[]` for partial failures)

get.description:
Cursor-paginated audit trail of pricing recommendations vs applied prices for a listing across a date window. Use `pagination.nextCursor` from one response as the `cursor` query param of the next request.

Defaults to ±90 days from today. Cursor is a keyset on `date ASC` — stable even if rows are added during a partner's pagination walk. `limit` is capped at 500 — exceeding returns 422.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Inclusive. Defaults to today - 90 days.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Inclusive. Defaults to today + 90 days.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned in the previous response's `pagination.nextCursor`. Omit to fetch the first page.

get.summary: Comp set for a listing (with daily nightly pricing)
get.description:
Returns the actual comp set for a listing — the underlying competitor listings (with daily nightly pricing), not just the aggregated `compSummary` from `/pricing`. Each comp comes back with distance, bedrooms, ratings, lat/lng, platform link, and a per-day rate/availability series for the requested window.

Powered by Atlas. Comps with no coordinates are excluded — there's no way to rank them by distance. Listings without coordinates return `data: []` and a `warning` field.

parameters.name: radius_km
parameters.description: Bbox + haversine on lat/lng. Default 5, max 50.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Closest-first. Max 100.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Defaults to today.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Defaults to today + 30 days.

get.summary: Atlas DNA segment intelligence for a listing
get.description:
Aggregates Atlas DNA segment signal (quality tier, design style, bedrooms) across the listing's geographic neighborhood (default: 5km radius) or the whole city, so consumers can answer:
- What segments dominate my market?
- Which segment does my listing match best?
- What's the ADR uplift for moving up a tier?

DNA coverage is still ramping — segments are scored asynchronously. Cities and radii without scored comps return `totalCompsAnalyzed: 0` plus a `low_dna_coverage` recommendation rather than fabricated data.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: `comp_set` (default) restricts to a `radius_km` bbox. `market` aggregates across the whole city.

parameters.name: radius_km
parameters.description: Only used when `level=comp_set`.

200.description: Segment intelligence (may carry a `low_dna_coverage` recommendation when scope is unscored)

get.summary: List markets the customer operates in
get.description: Returns per-city KPIs across every market the authenticated customer has listings in (market share, ADR vs market, occupancy, ratings) plus a lightweight `browse` discovery summary (top-50 featured markets, country categories, total catalog size). For the full paginated discovery catalog with search, call `GET /v1/markets/browse`. Each `markets[]` entry is enriched with `subscribed` + `source` from the customer's market subscriptions.

Cursor-paginated, search-filterable catalog of every Atlas-tracked market the customer could expand into. Backed by the precomputed `market_summaries` table (>=5 active comps per city). Supports fuzzy `q` substring search (trigram-indexed), `country` (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) filter, and `sort` (`listings_desc` | `name_asc`). Use the `nextCursor` from `pagination` to walk pages — the cursor is an opaque base64 token; do not parse it.

`pagination.total` is the count of markets matching the current `q`/`country`/`min_listings` filter (across all pages) — same shape as every other list endpoint.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Substring match on city name (case-insensitive).

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (e.g. `US`, `ES`).

parameters.name: min_listings
parameters.description: Minimum comp-set size — cities with fewer active comps are excluded.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned by the previous page's `pagination.nextCursor`.

get.description: Detailed market view for one city — price distribution, bedroom mix, property types, upcoming events, Wheelhouse demand, monthly benchmarks, customer health rollup, top comps (proximity-sorted, paginated), customer's percentile position, capacity-mix gap, and a 6-month supply trend.

parameters.name: city
parameters.description: URL-encoded city name (e.g. `Radium%20Hot%20Springs`).

parameters.name: compsPage
parameters.description: 1-indexed page number for the `topComps` slice.

get.description: Date-by-date market view for a city — market avg / min / max nightly rate, occupancy %, Wheelhouse demand, events touching the date, and (when `listingId` is supplied) an overlay of the customer's own pricing + availability.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Defaults to today.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Defaults to today + 365 days.

parameters.name: listingId
parameters.description: Optional — overlays the customer's own pricing/availability for direct comparison. Bypasses the upstream cache.

post.description:
Perform an AI-powered operation.

Operations:
- `respond-to-guest` — Generate a contextual guest response
- `classify-intent` — Classify the intent of a guest message
- `generate-listing` — Generate optimized listing description
- `review-response` — Generate a review response
- `price-suggestion` — Get AI pricing suggestions

get.description: Fetch the current plan, usage counters, and billing-cycle reset date for this workspace. Use this to surface a "you have used X / Y" indicator in your dashboard.

post.description: Redirect user to Stripe checkout.

Create a workspace-scoped field-mapping schema. The schema reshapes the `native` response payload into your app's preferred field names. After creation, set `X-Schema: <name>` on any read endpoint to apply it.

**Reserved names:** `calry`, `calry-v1`, `native` are built-in schemas and cannot be used as a custom name.

**Mapping safety:** Each mapping value is parsed by an internal expression engine — `eval`, `Function`, `process`, and other unsafe keywords are rejected up front. Field names are capped at 100 chars and expressions at 500 chars.

get.description: Returns every custom schema owned by the workspace, including inactive ones.
Fetch a single custom schema by id. Scoped to the authenticated workspace — schemas that belong to other workspaces return 404.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Custom schema id.

patch.description: Patch the description, mappings, or active flag of a custom schema. The schema `name` is immutable — create a new schema and migrate consumers if you need to rename. Mapping updates are revalidated for safety.

delete.description: Hard-delete a custom schema. Subsequent requests carrying its name in `X-Schema` fall back to `native`. There is no undelete.
